title: alnus tinctoria f. acutiloba
intro: Alnus tinctoria f. acutiloba, also known as a variety of the golden alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce pain, swelling, and infection. The health benefits of Alnus tinctoria f. acutiloba include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be dried and used in decoctions or added to capsules for medicinal purposes.
